title: The rights and responsibilities of citizenship: level 1
description: Test your knowledge on the rights, responsibilities, and obligations of citizenship!
topic_path: What are the rights and responsibilities of citizenship?
The rights and responsibilities of citizenship: level 1

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the definition of a right?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a freedom that allows people to do certain actions without fear of interference
a course of action that citizens must take as demanded by the government
an action or habit that prioritizes the collective good over an individual’s private interests
a shared moral and social foundation of civic societies

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the definition of personal rights?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the rights belonging to an individual to decide the best course of action for themselves
the rights to participate in the actions associated with governance
the rights to control your own welfare, as it relates to the conditions you work in and your ability to make money
the rights guaranteed to people at birth that the government cannot interfere with

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the definition of political rights?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the rights to participate in the actions associated with governance
the rights belonging to an individual to decide the best course of action for themselves
the rights to control your own welfare, as it relates to the conditions you work in and your ability to make money
the rights guaranteed to people at birth that the government cannot interfere with

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the definition of economic rights?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the rights to control your own welfare, as it relates to the conditions you work in and your ability to make money
the rights belonging to an individual to decide the best course of action for themselves
the rights to participate in the actions associated with governance
the rights guaranteed to people at birth that the government cannot interfere with

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the difference between a civic responsibility and an obligation of citizenship?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Civic responsibilities are activities that citizens should do, and obligations are things that citizens have to perform.
Civic responsibilities are activities that local governments require citizens to perform, and obligations are activities that the federal government requires.
Civic responsibilities are activities one does with their family to teach them about politics, and obligations are activities that citizens should perform.
There is no difference between civic responsibilities and obligations.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the Selective Service system?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a way for all eligible people to register for military service if there is ever a military draft
a way to determine who is eligible to serve as a public official in government
a way to match volunteers to organizations that work towards goals they are passionate about
a way to pair eligible Americans to their mandatory service job upon completing high school

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following groups must register for the Selective Service?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: all people assigned male at birth between the ages of eighteen and twenty-six
all people assigned female at birth under the age of forty
all people who are incarcerated from ages eighteen to twenty-six
all people over the age of twenty-six who have served in the military

assessment_items.question_content:
| personal right | political right
- | - | -
**A** | right to legally protect your own inventions | right to vote in elections
**B** | right to participate in political protests | right to access education
**C** | right to privacy | right to join a political party
**D**| right to worship how you choose | right to acquire property

**Which of the following pairings accurately compares personal and political rights?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Comparison A
Comparison B
Comparison C
Comparison D

assessment_items.question_content:
| political right | economic right
- | - | -
**A** | right to vote in elections | right to a fair trial by jury
**B** | right to hold elected office | right to join a labor union
**C** | right to decide where you live | right to access social security
**D**| right to freedom of speech | right to assemble peaceably

**Which of the following pairings accurately compares political and economic rights?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Comparison A
Comparison B
Comparison C
Comparison D

assessment_items.question_content:
| economic right | personal right
- | - | -
**A** | right to acquire property | right to decide where you live
**B** | right to organize for better working conditions | right to serve in the military when there is a Selective Service draft
**C** | right to choose where you work | right to run for elected office
**D**| right to vote | right to privacy

**Which of the following pairings accurately compares economic and personal rights?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Comparison A
Comparison B
Comparison C
Comparison D

assessment_items.question_content:
| political right | responsibility 
- | - | -
**A** | right to move to another country | participating in your local community
**B** | right to make decisions about your body | working for a living wage
**C** | right to refuse healthcare | performing public service
**D**| right to a lawyer when facing criminal proceedings | staying informed of current events

**Which of the following pairings accurately compares political rights and responsibilities?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Comparison A
Comparison B
Comparison C
Comparison D

assessment_items.question_content:
| economic right | obligation
- | - | -
**A** | right to petition the government | obey laws
**B** | right to represent yourself in contracts | serve on a political campaign
**C** | right to work in safe conditions | pay taxes
**D**| right to acquire, use, or dispose of property | volunteering in your community

**Which of the following pairings accurately compares economic rights and obligations?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Comparison A
Comparison B
Comparison C
Comparison D

assessment_items.question_content:
| personal right | obligation
- | - | -
**A** | right to access education | staying informed
**B** | right to information from the government | defend the country
**C** | right to own private property | running for federal office
**D**| right to move to another country | serve on a jury

**Which of the following pairings accurately compares personal rights and obligations?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Comparison A
Comparison B
Comparison C
Comparison D

assessment_items.question_content:
| economic right | responsibility
- | - | -
**A** | right to a speedy and fair trial by jury | voting in elections
**B** | right to earn a wage | staying informed
**C** | right to safe working conditions | running for office
**D**| right to enter lawful contracts | serving on a political campaign

**Which of the following pairings accurately compares economic rights and responsibilities?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Comparison A
Comparison B
Comparison C
Comparison D

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following are personal rights that citizens have?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: right to refuse medical treatment
right to move to another state
right to access education
right to work in safe conditions

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following are political rights that citizens have?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: right to vote in elections
right to hold elected office
right to access social security
right to choose where you work

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following are economic rights that citizens have?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: right to choose where you work
right to legally protect your inventions
right to join a labor union
right to a quick, fair trial by jury

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following are promises that the government expects citizens of the United States to keep?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: to defend the Constitution of the United States
to serve in the US military if called upon
to vote in elections at all levels of government
to join a labor union when one is available

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following are rights that only United States citizens have?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: vote in a federal election
run for federal office
serve in the military
pay income taxes

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following are obligations that citizens must complete?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: serve on a jury when called upon
pay federal, state, and local taxes
obey federal, state, and local laws
form opinions on political issues

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following are civic responsibilities that good citizens carry out?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: vote in elections
participate in their community
attend school until they’re adults
defend the United States when called upon
